rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Shinjuku, Shibuya, Shimokitazawa, Koenji); central 1BR ~JPY 140,000-180,000 (~$1,000-1,300); serviced apts ~JPY 200,000 ($1,500)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; Japan internet excellent ~JPY 5,000/mo; AC/heating costs add up

groceries.note: home cooking; supermarkets affordable for Japanese staples; JPY 30,000-40,000/mo

diningOut.note: Tokyo dining exceptional value at low end (ramen JPY 800-1,200); mid-range JPY 2,000-4,000/meal; convenient stores cheap

transit.note: 1-month commuter pass ~JPY 12,000-15,000 (~$80-100) depending on route; JR East fare revision +7.8% March 2026

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly JPY 30,000-50,000 premium locations; day pass JPY 1,200-2,200; mid estimate ~JPY 35,000

simData.note: SIM-only plan (IIJmio/Mineo/NTT Docomo sub-brands); unlimited data ~JPY 2,500-3,500/mo

misc.note: gym (~JPY 6,000), toiletries, entertainment, temples/shrines/culture, konbini runs, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Tokyo furnished lets are fairly stable; slight spring premium (cherry blossom) for short stays, not affecting monthly rentals.

evidence.claim: Tokyo remote worker monthly budget range
evidence.quote: For remote workers wanting comfort with a furnished apartment, regular eating out and occasional coworking, expect JPY 320,000 to JPY 450,000 per month
A one-bedroom apartment in the city center costs $1,000–$1,200; serviced apartments average around 200,000 yen per month (roughly $1,500)
digital nomads should plan for approximately $2,485/month covering coworking, accommodation, food, and local transport

evidence.claim: Tokyo commuter pass monthly cost after 2026 fare revision
evidence.quote: A 1-month commuter pass for the Tokyo to Kichijoji route costs 12,290 yen; JR East implemented a fare revision on March 14, 2026, with regular passenger fares rising by approximately 7.8%
